txt: 'Minime' stands for a 'mini-meeting'. It's part of a bigger framework for managing work called R.U.N. Station 41 tried to implement it a few years ago, but failed.
'Red Astra' is the black market version of 'Astra' cigarettes, known for their high tar content.
'So if I can shoot rabbits then I can shoot fascists too' -- those are the next lines.

'The Ikeira Operation' was a seven-year campaign during which Suzerain Guillaume's army forcefully united the people in the southeastern part of Le Petit Continent, collectively known as the Ikeira tribes, under the Revacholian banner.

'Truth Hunt' is a popular radio programme. Each week, its two eccentric hosts explore a variety of topics, from cryptids and conspiracy theories to ethnic warfare and the 'historical' Man from Hjelmdall. A radio counterpart to the Graadian fringe science magazine 'Paradox B'.
*Ahem*. It's *very* similar to the face Hjelmdallermann is always making on the cover of those books...
*Benafed* is children's cold medicine, usually apricot flavoured, and *risperizole* is used to treat severe psychosis.
*Fascha* -- that's short for fascist.
*Hepato-* means liver and *-biliary* the gallbladder and bile ducts.
*Kipt* is a pejorative term used to describe people of Semenese descent.
*Kipt* is a pejorative term used to describe people of South Semenese or Areopagite descent. It used to be a common first name among the Areopagites of Iilmaraa -- not so much anymore.
*Marche* -- On. *Arrêt* -- Off.
*Maun* is a derogative term for first-world people of Gottwaldian descent. They do not *all* have eczema. Also, people of Katla, like the Suru and the Uuhu, are much more lactose intolerant.

*Memento mori* or 'remember that you must die' is a slogan various religious orders have thrown around since the dawn of mankind, to emphasize the vanity of earthly life and the transient nature of all earthly pursuits. In essence it means one should live virtuously in this life to live better in the afterlife.
*Pig* is a widely-used term for members of the Police. It's not loving.
*Reservoirs*? No -- but do they take obscure trivia and odd titbits?
*Terrifying* is a term too emotionally charged for your semantic memory. Or what remains of it, but...
*The Setting Sun* was a decoration used to distinguish seasoned combat veterans in service of King Frissel the First during the Revolution.
*The board* is where the stockholders send their representatives. Often those representatives don't even represent the stockholders' interests. It's not actually like some council-based democracy.
*You*, sir -- you are the Copopedia.

... Well, in fairness, you haven't seen much more of the coast. But you do know one last thing: ULAN. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. ULAN, the Speedfreaks mystery -- it might just be related.
...Roberts, Feuerbach, De Mettrie... suddenly names from your décomptage flash in your forebrain.
.9998 repeating.
005 is Revachol ZoC -- 001 is Graad, on the Graadian isola, where the telephone was invented. The next two digits you dial are the area code for the city of Mirova...
850 million people live on these little dots. An oceanic world of culture and commerce, torn apart by history.
A *boiadeiro* (*boia* for short) is a cow herder from upstream Magritte, the great steppes of Northern Mesque. He is a rugged individualist and explorer.
A *boiadeiro* (*boia* for short) is a cow herder from upstream Magritte, the great steppes of Northern Mesque. People like Mañana, at the gates, have turned it into an ideology of sorts.
A 9mm bullet to be exact. Fit for all muzzleloaders of that calibre, like your own Villiers pistol, for example.
A 9mm bullet to be exact. Fit for all muzzleloaders of that calibre.
A Dolorian-era symbol of Insulinde. Known as The Face In The Sea.
A Stas-Rajko! One of the finest Zsiemsk-made motor carriages ever. An oldie but a goldie.
A child born with Moller's disease -- Harlequinism -- grown up miraculously.
A common accounting gimmick used to inflate precinct stats.
A curious octopus sometimes collects things? Or a cuttlefish?
A curious octopus would do something similar. Or even a cuttlefish.
A hint system is not part of the protocol for repeater stations.
A legendary -- and not in a *good* way -- crime boss from Jamrock. Controls what is probably the most powerful organised crime outfit in Revachol West.
A motor lorry, also called a *camion* on Caillou and neighbouring islands. This one looks roughed up enough to be some sort of a FALN rust-bucket, maybe the A-6?
A normal human being like Lieutenant Kitsuragi here needs to pause before they know whose shoe this is. Not you. You literally *feel* the association taking shape in your *torque* bent frontal lobe.
A paraplegic is someone with limited or no ability to use the lower half of their body. Paraplegia is caused by spinal cord injuries -- like falling from a great height, or a grenade explosion!
A pipo is a type of hat by the way.

A pop quiz is a short examination designed to test your knowledge without any prior warning or announcement. Such exams allow the teacher to assess how thoroughly the students have retained the material at hand -- voila! Now blast that first innocence!
A primitive nuclear reactor, also called a *pile*.
A short-lived legislative foundation for a short-lived utopia.
A silver bird... feels strangely familiar. Was it an expression? And if yes, then for what? Then your mind slips and the thought is gone.
A small steel plaque reads 'Halter 800'. Halter is a Königsteiner lift company who went out of business a long, long time ago.
A statement, seemingly contradictory or opposed to common sense, and yet perhaps true. Or perhaps not.
A studious approach... Seems like a good direction. Turn it into *science*!
A vague, blackened image doesn't sound like somewhere you can stay if you run out of money.
A white bear? A polar bear?
A-khm! This cop here solved 216 cases over 18 years.
A... billion?

AFTER LIFE -- DEATH; AFTER DEATH -- LIFE AGAIN. AFTER THE WORLD -- THE PALE; AFTER THE PALE -- THE WORLD AGAIN. This is the great leitmotiv of humanism, a summary of the effect of the discovery of this isola -- the Insulindian -- on human thinking. A tremendous sea change akin to finding life after death...
Absolutely no idea. A hundred years?
Across the rusty water -- that's La Delta, the financial district. In the mist-covered distance, towers rise as a rebuke to the poverty of this coast.
Actually, Revachol doesn't have a mayor...
Actually, neither ostriches or sheep bury their heads in the sand. It's just a saying.
Adapting a high-quality radio-transmitter to emit this frequency may make it powerful enough to transmit a signal from isola to isola. But that's just a guess.
Ah -- this sounds like epistemology. A field so occupied by thought that it begins to question thought itself.
Ah yes, 'protein mass' -- a scientific term from Graad, for a person terminally overexposed to the pale. Mouth open, catatonic, completely unable to return to the present.
Ah, yes, *phasmatodea* -- a diverse group of insects whose bodies resemble twigs, leaves, that sort of thing. "Ghost insects," colloquially.
All major cities in EPIS-aligned nations, you can't help but notice.
All mighty god! Even Dick Mullen only has a .45!
All right. We're in. I know these *all* look good, but begin with the first, okay.
All you know is, this is the young mother of humanity -- and that you should go. Do something else! Escape her sad, worryworn look.
Along with a number of more banal street names: Blow, of course, but also flake, powder, pearl. Really anything that's white will get the idea across.
Along with a number of more banal street names: Blow, of course, but also flake, powder, pearl. Really anything that's white will work.

Alright, here we go -- Ms. Kapaleva (née Olga Grebennikova) was once known as East Revachol's premier vedette and sex symbol. Her role in director Do Do Chakan's sexploitation motion picture 'Psychotronic Stripper' launched her into stardom. She's been voted Most Captivating Woman by La Fayette *three years* running.
Also, some social democrats were already using it.
Also, there is no such thing as a 'homo circus'.
Although it's difficult to say for sure -- the RCM *may* descend from the ICM. At some, dark point in the past...
Although she is often considered to be the greatest human being to ever live, there *was* something ominous about Dolores Dei -- constantly surrounded by her Therriers...
Amblyopia. A childhood condition.
An Ister AR-FA7, built for taking out light armoured vehicles. It will devastate you.
An advancement of the KR18GU engine of the Coupris 40, to be precise.
An array of delicious recipes flashes through your mind. Salads... Salmon... Sandwiches. Bingo!

An emergency valve defect resulted in steam pressure blowing the turbine, taking the fuel containment vessel up in the explosion. Both the faulty design and lack of finances contributed to the catastrophe.

An innocence is infallible. The decisions made by one are not decisions. They are inevitabilities -- what would have happened anyway, only accelerated, packed into decades instead of centuries. An Innocence is a continuous, compressed event, a sacred human being. It is an honour and a glory to live when one is in office.

An innocence is the highest category of historical personage in the world, a literal personification of History. Traditionally, an innocence, when anointed, assumes supreme rule over the Occident, or the known world in general -- at least the parts that matter.

An intricate system of irrigation networks pockmarking the earth, intermittent seas of phosphorous mud, ripped tarpaulin fluttering in the wind... A pair of molten rubber boots also comes to mind. All in all -- a truly hard core place.
Anal acrobats actually train using a series of stomach breathing exercises, not butt-clenches. Not many people know that.
And the 4,000-year-old Seraseolitic civilization buried so deep in the sediment that you do not even know it is there -- they too did not see me.

And, while you might not have seen much of the coast, you were probably right: The ULAN frequency is likely connected to all this. Ruby dabbles in advanced radio theories. The Speedfreaks FM case is a radio mystery. That may not be coincidence.
Another discrepancy, although not boot-related, is the coronation of His Innocence Franconegro. Which happened 500 years ago.
Apologies! You just need time to remember. There has to be something there. Go do other things around the coast. That might help.

As a matter of historical fact, this is almost *exactly* what happened in Safre. To this day, fully half of the former Safre Empire remains dependant on international aid in exchange for a steady supply of cheap produce.

As did we all. The lands of Mesque and the Occident and even far-away Supramundi -- altogether twenty one of the forty Mondial nations of the time -- immediately accepted innocentic rule. Even before her crowning.

As you read it you think: that leaves out dark haired Mesque, Sao, Saramirizian, Kedreatic.... Lighter skinned Occidental means this man could be... Vespertine? Oranjese? Messinian. Those three are on the table.
At least not under the influence of stimulants. That doesn't rule out: hallucinogens, benzos, some depressants... how do you know all this?
Averro, SRL, is a chain of high-end pharmacies found only in Vesper and Messina. They are *much* nicer than your average Frittte.
B-hydroxy-phenothiazine. Somewhere in the soggy remains of your factual memory, you know the name. From the streets.
Bad Hand Hermenegildo's *bad* hand strangled three hundred people. What can I say -- some people just really like strangling people.

Banaital is one of the inhabited islands of the Semenese archipelago, near the pale. Covered in  jungle. It was the anchor point for the Semenese nationalists -- in the proxy war held on the islanders' territory.
Barbara Muskova and Numb 12, Joyce Messier's choices...
Because white is the colour of peace.
Beneath the cold waters of the Insulindian Bay, thrown there by the revolutionaries after they cleaned out the royal mausoleum.
Besmertnyé or the Besmertie -- the *immortals* -- are West Revacholian crime syndicates.
Best of all, your arguments are liberally seasoned with references to particular historical events and personages.
Betancourt got bombed in the war. It used to be quite near. Circa 20 kilometres from here.
Big red key? That's code for the battering ram. Cop talk. You know this.
Bowers are built by males of species who can't afford colourful mating displays physically -- this one *was* plain coloured...
Buckle up!
But to what? There is almost nothing.
But wait! You haven't even heard about his fabled cocaine addiction.
But you've been at this hostel-cafeteria for only three nights. Where were you before? You had to be somewhere.
But... she was not hell. She is the opposite of that, the wind of paradise, stripping away the covers from the hearts of men...
By 'primo' he means 'possibly carcinogenic'. Lickra(TM) was a synthetic fabric developed by  manufacturers in the middle '40s, notable mainly for the swishy sound it makes as the wearer passes by...
By introducing cocaine into Safre under exclusive contract, the suzerain created an extremely valuable captive market for an extremely *addictive* product.
By the way! Dolorian tragedies are still held in high regard for innovation in language and motifs. They are also know for overwrought emotions leading to catastrophic outcomes.
By the way, 'kipt' is a racial slur.

By the way, Sam Bo or Samaran boxing is an eloquently violent set of one-on-one fighting moves originating from the Samaran isola. Sam Bo used to incorporate a wide array of martial disciplines from archery to mounted combat, but mainly means aesthetically pleasing single combat nowadays. *Sam Bo style* implies stealth, cleverness, and cool.

By the way, the raw materials were most likely exported from Siigay (the Apricot Suzerainty) and processed in Sur-La-Clef into the apricot-flavoured chewing gum loved by kids of today -- and yesterday. Hmm. Something about it *is* familiar... And not only to your fingers.
By the way, where *is* home? The address is coming up blank and this place sure isn't it...
Bystanders reported golden filaments lighting the already sunlit chamber around her, clearly visible beneath her dress. I repeat: *Her lungs started glowing*. Do you care now?
Bystanders reported golden filaments lighting the already sunlit chamber around her, clearly visible beneath her dress.
Called Iilmaraa. And it didn't. Even after the Mesque colonized it seven ways from Sunday it's an industrial power house.
Chaise électrique is the method of capital punishment in Revachol under the Coalition. During the Suzerain's reign it used to be the firing squad.

Clinging to a picture-book version of the past century, waiting for the King to come back and cast out all the profiteers and homo-sexuals. Basically, imagine a yellow plastic crown with a liquor brand emblazoned on it.

Co Hoi, a village that was the site of infamous war crimes in the South Safre conflict, the ongoing proxy war between Graad and Safre. It has been hot for 12 years, with atrocities piling up daily. Mostly committed by Graad.
Coalition Warship Archer can shoot fifty shells a minute, on twenty co-aligned arches. They will reach the city in 58 seconds.
Cocaine.
Communism is the ideology of gutless intellectuals and clueless university students. They don't care about the *real* wörking man.
Construction began during the Commune of Revachol. The *people* continued work on it after the Commune fell -- they wanted a cheap source of energy for Revachol West.

Contemporary Revacholians prefer Drouin (a local blend from the southern islands) or Astra, the legendary cigarette from Graad. Tioumoutiri is favoured by older men for its paper filter tips, sweet smell, and added tar.

Cornelius Gurdi was a Mesque mountaineer, travel-writer and a national hero, known for his daring expeditions into the far corners of the world and perhaps even more daring statements in the public arena. He never returned from his final conquest -- Corpus Mundi.
Corpus Mundi, the dormant shield volcano on the Mundi isola is indeed the highest peak in the known world. At the height of 18.9 km it almost reaches stratosphere.
Correction: four-thousand-three-hundred-and-ninety-five. The fourth largest public protest of a criminal trial in Revachol.
Couron is just east of Jamrock. It was a short walk, every morning -- to the baseball field or the sports building...
Cyanoacrylate *is* super glue.
Damn.

Deora was one of the three *Crown Cities* of the Dolorian era, on the Mundi isola -- the others being Rheasilvia and Advesperascit. This theme is all about early airships and beautiful, sad, pearl-laden women. It's quite nice actually.

Despite the damage you've done to yourself, the title appears lodged in your hippocampus. This is Her Innocence Dolores Dei -- the innocence of humanism, internationalism, and the welfare state -- perhaps the most famous human being ever to have lived.
Dick Mullen is *not* your name. It's the name of a *fictional* detective who would *not* lose his badge.
Did you know ethyl ether is used as a light anaesthetic? And a solvent. And an explosive. And for manufacturing amphetamine.

Dolores Dei was shot in the chest with a fowling piece, eight times. The man, thought to be insane, said he once touched her and her body had been unnaturally warm, like a furnace -- and that sometimes while on duty he observed her forgetting to breathe for over ten minutes...
Don't be a fool -- as an expert, it's your duty to tell what you know to everyone.
Don't worry, it's (mostly) all still here. You can remember things. You *got* this.
Drouamine. Then it's not *that* bad. Neither surgical, nor organ-damage bad. But still under-the-counter bad.
Easy! Everybody knows the answer to this. You, me, anybody.
Entertain the thought when you get the time. Maybe being a hobo *and* a cop has its advantages?

Entrapment is a practice whereby an officer of the law induces a person to commit a criminal offence that the person would have otherwise been unlikely to commit. Usually through some trickery, persuasion or fraud.
Even by the standards of the Filippian kings, Old Sumptuous Filippe was known for his profligacy.
Even today, half a century after, the star-and-antlers retains the ability to evoke hope, disappointment, and fear in equal measure.
Everyone loved it. Maybe you thought some of the stardust would rub off on you? Maybe it did? Either way, it's all gone now. Only the grimace remains.
Evrart said there were two thousand three hundred and seventy two Union members. But whatever keeps their spirits up.
Exactly. That's what he's known as. His reputation proceeds him!
Fair to olive skinned, from the isola of Mundi. This is as vague as it gets. You might as well say: white-ish.
Feld Electrical -- you only know them as a small company that makes ink cartridges. Looks like they used to be... big.
Fish is sometimes used to fertilize the soil a few weeks before planting something. Maybe she was preparing  the garden beds?
For Revachol -- your city -- that meant only one thing: Guillaume Le Million!

For some reason your mind is a veritable repository of Guillaume le Million lyrics, trivia, and b-sides. But the time to explore this is when you're alone, facing a mirror perhaps? Right now it would derail the reality low-down.
For the Northwest Region of Revachol, an officer's average yearly income is 5,500 reál (unadjusted for rank).

From recordings. Of the far pale. You've heard them. We all have.
From the looks of it this is a traditional Semenese ward, meant to provide protection against ill luck, bad dreams, curses, and other supra-natural scourges.

Funk is an aggressive resurgence of *The New* aesthetic of the Revacholian Thirties, but this time originating in Graad. It's brought there by the Iilmaraan diaspora. Funk is more masculine and guitar-oriented than disco. Even more *dangerous*.
Glad to share! The value of the motor carriage has been seriously diminished by its colour: all Stas-Rajkos are baby-blue by default. The paint would be considered unforgivable to collectors.

Glad you asked. When junior researcher Õlari Taal invented eternite in the Värtna Polytechnic Institute, some thirty odd years ago, he thought it would last forever -- hence the name eternite. Sadly the only lasting thing turned out to be the material's highly carcinogenic effect.
Goat milk butter. The Samaran terrain makes it easier to raise goats than cows, and the local goat farmers produce high-quality meat and dairy staples that add variety to other nations' diets.
Gone. Gone is the glory. Of hope, only the scribblings of impoverished students remain. In dirty hallways...
Gossamer is a fine, filmy substance consisting of cobwebs spun by small spiders, seen especially in autumn. It's safe to say he's referring to the near absence of regulations.
Got it under control. No problem. Solid on this one. It's wide-spread historical information.
Great!
Hah! Hydrotransducers. So that's what those water basins are -- devices for recording sound through water.
Harmel-Rioux 'Supersonique' -- a racing motorbike for the road -- could probably do it.

He means The New, the third decade of the current century. The decade of disco, decadence, and the victory of democracy-powered free market economy over it's critics.
He means asexual reproduction. The females of the species don't need to mate to produce viable eggs. This makes it easier for a species with a small population to survive.
He spent his youth in Villalobos, a housing project in the Jamrock Quarter. There were incarcerations. Hard to say what else -- the ink is fading.
He's basically admitting to it.
He's from Graad. People from Graad don't mind if they're called by their national derogatives. It's okay to call him a *Kojko*.
He's right. Almost no one remembers there was a third metastasis of the World Revolution, in the Safre Empire, extinguished in '06.
Her accent suggests she is not from around here. She's from Oranje, another part of the world.
Her nonchalance *might* be related to something called the Wayfarer Act -- a law that says she doesn't *need* a licence. Sly fox... you're not aggressive enough to harass her further on this.
Here it is again, this pale... If only you knew more about it.
Highly unusual. The people of Revachol haven't carried breech-loading weapons like this for nearly half a century.

Igaunija -- a god-forsaken (and tiny) territory in Graad that has not once, but twice wasted its independence on microfascism. The less said about Igaunija the better. Matter of fact, let's stop saying things about it right now and continue what we were doing.
Ignorance is bliss, as they say. Go ahead, answer the man!

In a city called Advesperascit, in Vesper-Messina, her homeland. The name of the city means 'Evening comes,' but it happened on a winter's morning with the canals frozen and slush falling out of the sky.

In a way. An innocence is elected to office by the Founding Party, a precedent that has taken place a mere six times in the entirety of History. The legal system of the Reál Belt is built from the ground up to accommodate innocentic rule, should it coincide with our time.

In fact, Yekokataa is a desolate wasteland whose name literally translates to *Zone of Ecological Catastrophe*. It features no scenic vistas and supports virtually no plant or animal life.

In the Free State of Semenine. Hidden away at the edge of the Earth, near the pale.

In time or space?

Incidentally, 'The Saddest Pig in the World' is the title of a popular Gottwaldian children's book, written and illustrated by Moritz Metzger. In '26 it received the prestigious *Kritikerpreis* for youth literature.

It's a Graad name -- Zsiemsk or Yugo-Graad in origin -- not Occidental at all. Smells of motor oil, taiga, economic desolation, and rock music infused alcoholism.

It's an optical atmospheric anomaly the first settlers saw. Happens in cold weather: six small suns around the big one. This complex halo-phenomena is how old Revachol got its flag.
It's clearly some manner of super-armour. Or future armour? Super-future armour? I'm useless.

It's one of the foundational laws in Revachol, designed to limit the authority of local institutions. Part of it prohibits the RCM from requesting most documents and licenses without permission from a Coalition representative...

The Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient is a ratio designed to reflect the difference in net worth between individuals. When the coefficient is close to 1 (or 100%) it means one person possesses all the net worth among that group of individuals.
